stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 50 subscribers. The sample mean is 243.7 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 34.2 Mbps. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 243.7 Mbps. 95% CI = [234.0, 253.4]. Point estimate: 243.7 Mbps.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
